title: erysimum lanceolatum var. major
intro: Erysimum lanceolatum var. major, also known as lance-leaved wallflower, is a perennial plant in the Brassicaceae family, native to the mountainous regions of western North America. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been traditionally used to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The lance-leaved wallflower is rich in vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants, offering various health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and protecting against oxidative stress. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil for medicinal purposes, and is also used in herbal remedies for its expectorant and antispasmodic properties.
